## Internal cleanup: shared crate rename + log dedup

A maintenance release. No user-facing behavior changes, no config
changes, no wire-format changes. If you're running v0.5.1 there's
no functional reason to upgrade — distros may want to pick this
up to reduce drift.

## What changed

### `sentinel-config` → `sentinel-shared`

The internal workspace crate that holds shared code between the
PAM module, polkit agent, and helper has been renamed. The old
name was misleading — only ~30% of the crate is config; the rest
is `/proc` readers, logind metadata, the `Outcome` wire enum,
logfmt helpers, and shared path constants used by 2–3 of the
sibling crates.

This is a workspace-internal rename. It does **not** affect:

- `/etc/security/sentinel.conf` (filename + schema unchanged)
- The PAM module name (`pam_sentinel.so`)
- The polkit agent binary name (`sentinel-polkit-agent`)
- The helper binary (`sentinel-helper`)
- The Debian/RPM/Arch package names
- Any environment variable (`SENTINEL_CONFIG_PATH`, etc.)

Downstream packagers who do `cargo build --workspace` need no
changes.

### `pam_sentinel` log dedup

The `event=auth.{allow,deny,timeout,error}` audit lines from the
PAM module's dialog path were emitted by four nearly-identical
log statements. Collapsed into a single `match` on the outcome.
Same output, ~30 fewer lines, easier to keep the format string in
sync next time it changes.

### `pam_sentinel` locale env reader

`crates/pam-sentinel/src/locale.rs` had its own bespoke walker
over `/proc/<pid>/environ` that duplicated logic already in the
shared `procfs` module. Switched to calling
`sentinel_shared::procfs::read_environ_var` once per forwarded
locale variable. The whitelist validation
(`is_safe_locale_value`) is unchanged — that's the security
boundary and stays in `pam-sentinel`.

## Verify

Same as v0.5.1; behavior is identical:

```bash
pgrep -af sentinel-polkit-agent
pkexec true
journalctl -t sentinel-polkit-agent -t pam_sentinel \
    --output=cat --since "1 minute ago" \
    | grep "event=auth"
```

## Wiki

Full installation, configuration, architecture, and troubleshooting
docs at <https://github.com/atayozcan/sentinel/wiki>.
